title: Germany's KfW to Provide $25 Bln Loan for Hydrogen Network
content: KfW (KFW.UL) will provide a 24 billion euro ($25 billion) loan to help develop a future hydrogen network in Germany, the state lender told Reuters, at a time when companies have warned that the technology will take longer than expected to take off. Germany is betting on hydrogen, which can be used in part to replace natural gas, as it seeks to decarbonise the economy and find ways to absorb intermittent renewable supplies into the power grid.
author: Analysts Account
